articles.title: ICICI Bank gains while HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra, Yes Bank decline after Q1 results
articles.excerpt:
Shares of private sector banks witnessed pressure after the announcement of their June quarter earnings, with ICICI Bank emerging as a gainer among major lenders while HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank and Yes Bank declined.

In today’s session, ICICI Bank shares rose 1 per cent to ₹1461 on the BSE after reporting healthy earnings growth backed by higher net interest income, non-interest income and lower provisions.

In contrast, HDFC Bank fell over 5 per cent to ₹777.65, Axis Bank declined 5.48 per cent to ₹1256.10, Kotak Mahindra Bank slipped 2 per cent to ₹382.05.

In addition, Yes Bank reacted negatively, declining about 2.96 per cent, while Punjab National Bank increased 5.62 pe

articles.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares fall over 3% despite Q1 profit growth. Analysts weigh in
articles.excerpt:
Kotak Mahindra Bank shares fell after reporting a strong profit increase for the first quarter. Net profit rose 26% year-on-year, and net interest income also saw growth. Asset quality improved from the previous year, though key ratios weakened sequentially. Brokerages provided varied ratings, with some reiterating buy recommendations and others maintaining add ratings. Analysts cited controlled slippages and credit costs as positive factors supporting the bank's performance.

articles.title: Global Market: Euro Zone firms see slower price and wage growth, ECB survey shows
articles.excerpt:
Euro zone companies expect slower growth in selling prices and wages over the next year, according to the ECB's latest survey, signalling that inflationary pressures remain contained despite higher energy costs. The findings strengthen expectations that the central bank may keep interest rates unchanged this week.

articles.title: India bonds steady after swinging with oil
articles.excerpt:
On Monday, Indian government bonds remained stable as crude oil prices dipped, driven by renewed optimism surrounding U.S.-Iran negotiations. The benchmark 2036 bond yield held steady, mirroring previous trading patterns. Foreign investors returned to the Indian bond market, seizing opportunities at elevated yields following earlier sell-offs. However, lingering concerns about the U.S.-Iran situation and a potential monsoon deficit continue to weigh on investor sentiment.

India paid 60% more for crude oil imports in the April-June quarter compared to the same period last year, as the surge in oil prices couldn’t offset slightly lower import volumes.

articles.title: Global Market: German bond yields hit two-year high as oil surge fuels ECB rate hike bets
articles.excerpt:
German bond yields rose as oil prices increased amid U.S.-Iran tensions. This surge strengthened expectations for the European Central Bank to tighten monetary policy. Money markets now anticipate higher deposit rates by early 2027. Traders have fully priced in a rate hike at the ECB's upcoming September meeting. Italy's 10-year bond yield also increased, widening the spread with German yields.

articles.title: India 10-year bond slumps to lowest in nearly a month as Middle East oil risks heat up
articles.excerpt:
Indian government bonds experienced a significant decline early Monday. Escalating U.S.-Iran tensions pushed oil prices higher, impacting the rupee. This situation prompted traders to reduce their positions in government bonds. Higher crude prices strain India's import bill and government finances. Foreign investor demand for Indian debt is expected to moderate following recent inflows.

articles.title: ICICI beats HDFC on growth, margins in Q1. Is the lead sustainable?
articles.excerpt:
This is a Mint Premium article gifted to you.Subscribe to enjoy similar stories.

The June quarter (Q1FY27) earnings of private banking heavyweights ICICI Bank and HDFC Bank show the former pulling further ahead of its larger rival on loan growth, margins and return ratios.

ICICI reported nearly 20% year-on-year loan growth to ₹16.3 trillion, comfortably ahead of the industry's 18.6% credit growth (as of 30 June) and HDFC's 16% growth to ₹30.4 trillion.

Its net interest margin (NIM) expanded 4 basis points sequentially to 4.36%, though primarily aided by higher interest on tax refunds. Net profit grew 16% year-on-year to ₹14,800 crore, while return on assets (ROA) improved from 2.4% in Q4
articles.citation: ICICI beats HDFC on growth, margins in Q1. Is the lead sustainable? — Mint Markets, 2026-07-20. https://www.livemint.com/market/mark-to-market/icici-bank-hdfc-bank-q1fy27-loan-growth-nim-roa-casa-ldr-deposits-banking-rajiv-kumar-sashidhar-jagdishan-11784525565979.html

articles.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Dharmesh Shah of ICICI Sec suggests buying Piramal Pharma on 20 July
articles.excerpt:
Indian benchmark indices opened lower on Monday, 20 July, as weakness in HDFC Bank offset gains in select heavyweight stocks, while rising crude oil prices amid escalating tensions in the Middle East kept investors on edge.

The BSE Sensex declined 0.72% to 77,587.23, while the Nifty 50 slipped 0.60% to 24,185.80 in early trade.

HDFC Bank dropped nearly 5% after reporting its June-quarter results, as investors reacted to a sharper-than-expected sequential decline in net interest margins despite a 5% year-on-year increase in quarterly profit.

The weakness in the country's largest private lender overshadowed gains in Reliance Industries and ICICI Bank, both of which advanced around 1% after

articles.excerpt:

AI-driven gains remain under scrutiny after a chip stock selloff, while rising Middle East tensions and oil prices keep sentiment cautious.

Brent crude prices crossing $90 per barrel have reignited inflation concerns, potentially influencing Federal Reserve policy decisions. Market pricing suggests a relatively low probability of a rate hike in July, but expectations for September remain elevated.
